items.title: India's March palm oil imports fall to 3-month low as prices surge
items.summary: March brought a sharp turn for India's edible oil import scene, with palm oil imports witnessing a striking 19% decrease, marking a three-month low. items.title: UPI growth stays relentless with 22.64 billion March transactions, says Department of Financial Services
items.summary: Unified Payments Interface, or UPI, achieved 22.64 billion transactions in March 2026. This shows continued growth in digital payments across India. The total transaction value also increased significantly. UPI's daily transaction count and value saw a rise. Digital payments experienced strong year-on-year growth. India is transforming how it transacts.

New government rules and schemes like UDAY and RDSS are driving this improvement. Discoms are also paying current bills on time. This is bringing financial discipline to the sector.

items.summary: Indian refined sunflower oil demand is expected to fall about 10% in fiscal 2027 due to supply-chain disruptions from the Middle East conflict and higher prices, says Crisil. items.summary: India's indirect tax collections for fiscal year 2025-26 have surpassed the government's target. Customs, excise, and GST revenues showed strong performance. items.title: SpaceX files to go public, paving way for record stock offering
items.summary: The confidential filing puts the rocket and satellite builder on track to list its shares on a public exchange by July, according to The Wall Street Journal

items.title: US Stock Market | Global uncertainty tests investor confidence as traditional strategies falter
items.summary: Investor unease is rising as macroeconomic and geopolitical risks cloud visibility entering the second quarter. Despite a late equity rebound, markets posted weak quarterly performance. Concerns over inflation, policy uncertainty and diversification failures are prompting cautious behaviour, with advisers warning of potential downturn risks and shifting investor sentiment across global asset classes.

items.title: RBL Bank gets RBI nod for Emirates NBD stake buy; Sebi approval awaited
items.summary: RBL Bank has secured crucial approval from the Reserve Bank of India for its deal with Emirates NBD Bank. This clearance is expected to initiate a mandatory open offer to shareholders as early as next week. Emirates NBD Bank is set to acquire a majority stake in RBL Bank, marking a significant foreign investment in an Indian lender.

items.title: Gold rate today under pressure, oil and US dollar climb as Trump's speech shatters ceasefire hopes in US-Iran war
items.summary: The MCX gold rate today opened downward at  ₹1,52,490 and touched an intraday low of  ₹1,50,480 per 10 gm, losing over  ₹3,000 within a few minutes of the Opening Bell

items.title: Vijay Kedia’s FY26 scorecard: Losses dominate with 14 stocks falling up to 68%; SME multibagger shines
items.summary: Ace investor Vijay Kedia's portfolio saw substantial losses in FY26. Fourteen stocks dropped significantly, with some falling over 68 percent. Market challenges impacted mid and small-cap segments. However, TechD Cybersecurity emerged as a multibagger, delivering a 159 percent return. Advait Energy and Yatharth Hospital also showed strong gains.

items.title: CBIC waives full customs duty on critical petrochemical products
items.summary: Exemption to benefit sectors dependent on petrochemical feedstock and intermediates, including plastics, packaging, textiles, pharmaceuticals, chemicals, automotive components and other manufacturing segments

items.title: Sensex, Nifty plunge over 1.8% as crude surges on Trump’s Iran war threat
items.summary: The catalyst was Trump’s prime-time address, in which he declared that the US would hit Iran “extremely hard” in the coming weeks

items.title: Global market rattled as Trump signals longer Iran conflict
items.summary: Global markets are cautious. U.S. President Trump indicated the conflict with Iran may last longer. This news has dampened investor sentiment. Equity markets reacted negatively. Safe-haven demand supported the U.S. dollar. Oil prices moved higher due to supply disruption concerns. Investors are now focused on the uncertainty surrounding the conflict's duration.

items.title: RBI fresh FX curbs trap banks in trades once seen low risk
items.summary:
The move was aimed primarily at halting a surge in
corporate-driven arbitrage activity seen on Monday

items.title: Stocks to buy or sell: Osho Krishan of Angel One suggests buying Hindustan Zinc, Chennai Petroleum shares to buy
items.summary: The Nifty 50 and Sensex started lower on April 2, influenced by declines in Asian markets and U.S.-Iran tensions. Nifty 50 fell 1.31% and Sensex 1.19%. FPIs sold shares totaling 83.31 billion rupees, while DII purchased stocks worth 71.72 billion rupees, indicating mixed investor sentiment.

items.title: Nifty closer to fair valuations after 11% March crash but is it cheap enough for you to buy?
items.summary: Nifty and Sensex rebounded sharply to start the new financial year, following an 11% crash in March due to Middle East tensions. Analysts suggest Nifty's current P/E ratio of 20x indicates fairer valuations, potentially nearing a bottom. However, caution is advised as sustained rallies depend on cooling crude prices and FII inflows.

items.title: ₹1.22 lakh crore outflows! Is retail investors’ love for SIPs giving FIIs an easy exit route amid market turmoil?
items.summary: FIIs were net sellers on every single trading session in March, which tells you this was not tactical profit-booking but a structural reallocation away from India and emerging markets more broadly.

items.title: Silver crashes Rs 14,000/kg, gold dips Rs 2,600/10 grams as Trump's plan to hit Iran ‘very hard’ reignites inflation fears. What’s next?
items.summary: Gold and silver prices on MCX opened sharply lower Thursday following U.S. President Trump's threats of continued military action in Iran. Brent crude surged, while Treasury yields and the dollar index rose, further pressuring precious metals. Experts advise booking profits on rallies and avoiding fresh long positions.

items.title: Rs 11 lakh cr wiped out! Sensex slumps over 1,500 points, Nifty below 22,250: 5 factors behind today’s market crash
items.summary: Indian stock markets experienced a significant downturn on Thursday. The Sensex plummeted by almost 1,400 points, while the Nifty fell below 22,250. This sharp selloff erased substantial market capitalization. Investors reacted to global factors, including comments hinting at potential escalations in the Iran-US conflict. The market's performance reversed previous day's gains.

items.title: Sensex crashes 1500 points, investors lose  ₹10 lakh crore after Trump's speech— Key factors behind market fall explained
items.summary: The Sensex and the Nifty 50 crashed more than 2% each during the session.

items.title: From assembly to ambition: How India is building its electronics backbone
items.summary: India is shifting from electronics assembly to full-scale manufacturing as the ECMS scheme boosts domestic component production. With rising investments, localisation targets, and strategic focus on critical inputs like rare earth magnets, the sector offers a long-term opportunity, though execution and capacity ramp-up over the next few years remain key risks.

items.title: Amazon's cloud business in Bahrain damaged in Iran strike: Report
items.summary: Amazon’s cloud computing operation in Bahrain was damaged after ​an Iranian strike, the Financial Times ‌reported

items.title: Rupee jumps 2%, rises above 93 for first time in nearly 2 weeks after RBI measures
items.summary: The Indian rupee surged in early trading following the Reserve Bank of India's new measures to curb speculation and arbitrage flows. These actions are expected to lead to increased onshore dollar sales as traders unwind positions. Meanwhile, rising energy prices, driven by Middle East conflict concerns and U.S. President Trump's remarks, add to existing economic pressures.

items.title: Silver rate today crashes over  ₹13,000 as Donald Trump's speech lifts crude oil and dollar
items.summary: Silver rates fell nearly 6% on April 2 after President Trump's address heightened geopolitical tensions, affecting precious metals. MCX silver price decreased to  ₹2,29,888 per kg, while gold dropped to  ₹1,51,161 per 10 grams amid rising oil prices and a stronger dollar.

items.title: Forex math: Why weak rupee could be biggest growth driver for IT stocks this Q4 result season
items.summary: India's IT stocks are finding an unexpected boost from a weaker rupee, which analysts predict will cushion margins this Q4 results season. Despite flat revenue growth and AI concerns, currency tailwinds are expected to offset pressures, offering a near-term relief for the sector.

items.title: Emerging market rankings: India climbs to second spot in February, China reclaims crown
items.summary: India climbs to second position in February on strong domestic momentum, while China tops the rankings on a sharp export surge and Brazil jumps to third.

items.title: ETMarkets Smart Talk | FII comeback will be key trigger for next rally in Indian markets: Saibal Ghosh
items.summary: Foreign investor inflows are expected to fuel the next market surge. India's long-term growth story remains strong despite recent volatility. A cooling of global AI hype could draw foreign funds back. Investors should focus on domestic growth, particularly banking and financial services. Gold offers a hedge against uncertainty, with structural allocation advised long-term.

items.title: Ashok Leyland among 4 F&O stocks that saw a high increase in futures open interest
items.summary: There was a notable surge in futures open interest for nine stocks in the NSE F&O pack, contributing to a total increase in open interest of over 10% as compared to the previous trade.

items.title: Stock market today: Gift Nifty crashes after Trump's speech; US-Iran war to oil, gold, silver rates — 7 stocks to buy
items.summary: Stock market today: Experts recommend seven stocks to buy today; here're their buying range and profit-booking levels

items.title: TVS Motor shares fall 3% despite March sales jumping 25%, three-wheeler sales rocket 46%
items.summary: TVS Motor Company share price fell 3% on Thursday. This happened even as the company reported a strong 25% sales increase for March 2026. The stock drop was linked to global market worries. The West Asia conflict involving the US, Israel, and Iran created investor unease. Despite this, TVS Motor saw significant growth across its two-wheeler and three-wheeler segments.

items.title: Gold falls 1.3%, snaps 4-day rally after Trump’s Iran warning
items.summary: Spot gold ‌fell 1.3% at $4,694.48 per ounce while US gold futures slid 1.9% to $4,723.70

items.title: IDBI Bank among 4 midcap stocks flashing bullish signals, hinting at a possible uptrend
items.summary: Four NSE midcap stocks, including IDBI Bank, signalled bullish momentum after forming a White Marubozu pattern on April 1, indicating strong buying interest. Technical analysts view this pattern as a potential sign of continued upside, with traders closely watching these stocks for near-term trend confirmation and possible breakout opportunities.

items.title: Varun Beverages shares decline 3%; firm announces record date for Rs 0.50 final dividend
items.summary: Varun Beverages shares dipped 3% amid market weakness due to the West Asia war. The company set April 8 as the record date for its Rs 0.50 final dividend, payable from April 10. Despite a subdued 2025, structural drivers like retail expansion and portfolio diversification are expected to support long-term growth.

items.title: IndiGo shares tumble 5% as airline hikes fuel charges from today amid surging ATF costs
items.summary: IndiGo has raised fuel charges on domestic and international flights from April 2 as ATF prices surge sharply amid global supply disruptions. The move is expected to support margins but may impact travel demand. InterGlobe Aviation shares will remain in focus as rising fuel costs pressure the aviation sector’s profitability.

items.title: Gold Silver Rate Today Live Updates (2 April): Silver tumbles 6%, gold slips up to 3% on MCX after Trump dashes hopes of Iran war de-escalation
Tata Power shares in focus after Mundra plant restarts following nearly nine-month pause
items.summary: Tata Power has restarted operations at its Mundra thermal plant after a nearly nine-month shutdown, following revised power purchase agreements. The move is expected to support generation amid rising demand. While recent financial performance remains mixed, the plant’s revival strengthens the company’s operational outlook and supply commitments across multiple states.

items.title: HPCL, BPCL, IOCL shares plunge 4% as crude oil price jumps 5% to above $105. What are experts saying?
items.summary: Hindustan Petroleum, Indian oil, and Bharat Petroleum shares dropped 4% each on Thursday after crude oil prices jumped over five percent, exceeding one hundred five dollars a barrel. This surge followed a speech by US President Donald Trump. His remarks heightened worries about a prolonged Middle East conflict. The companies face pressure as their input costs rise significantly.

items.summary: Six Nifty 200 stocks that rallied over 4% on April 1 appeared in the RSI Trending Up scan, with their RSI crossing above 50 from lower levels. items.title: Centre waives duties on key petrochemical products amid supply disruptions due to Iran war
items.summary: India has waived import duties on crucial chemicals like ammonium nitrate, methanol, and PVC for three months, starting April 2nd. This move aims to ease supply chains and potentially lower costs for various industries. The government has also exempted ammonium nitrate from the Agriculture Infrastructure and Development Cess during this period, offering further relief.

items.title: Crude oil jumps over 4% after Trump says US to keep up attacks on Iran
items.summary: Brent crude futures rose $4.88, or 4.8%, ‌to $106.04 per barrel by 0200 GMT. U.S. ‌West ⁠Texas Intermediate crude futures were up $4.17, ⁠or 4.2%, to $104.29 per barrel

items.title: Stocks slide, dollar advances after Trump speech on Iran war
items.summary: The prospect of the end to the month-long ‌U.S.-Israeli war with Iran has lifted global stocks and knocked the dollar off its recent ‌highs in the past two sessions ‌after ⁠a brutal March where soaring oil prices ⁠sent risk assets into a tailspin

items.title: So what if the WTO is wobbly? Don’t dismiss the potential of plurilateral pacts among willing nations
items.summary: The WTO’s latest ministerial huddle ended with nothing to show. As smaller coalitions push ahead, India should rethink its resistance to plurilateral pacts. These could serve our interests well. Think of dispute resolution and investment facilitation.

items.title: RBI tightens forex derivatives norms to steady rupee amid volatility
items.summary: The Reserve Bank of India has tightened foreign exchange rules. Banks can no longer offer non-deliverable forward contracts to clients. This move aims to support the Indian rupee. The new regulations also prevent banks from rebooking derivative contracts and dealing with related parties. These measures are effective immediately and could impact banks and corporate treasuries.

items.title: Oil prices are still high. Why that could actually help stocks and lower inflation.
items.summary: Elevated oil prices tied to the Iran war are weighing on growth, but 22V Research says the slowdown in demand could help lower inflation and extend the economic cycle.

items.title: India's economy puts up brave front as GST, auto sales and UPI payments surge despite Gulf war
items.summary: India's economy shows remarkable resilience, with strong March figures in GST collections, vehicle sales, and digital payments, defying disruptions from the Gulf war. Despite a robust fiscal year-end, economists caution that rising costs and potential supply issues could soon impact the economy more visibly.

items.title: TVS family rift spills into Sundaram Clayton boardroom: Venu Srinivasan takes governance charge, tells Lakshmi to focus on biz
items.summary: Tensions within the TVS Group promoter family, involving Venu Srinivasan and his daughter Lakshmi Venu, have spilled into the boardroom at Sundaram Clayton, raising governance concerns. The board met twice in quick succession to first accept and then reverse the resignation of the company secretary, amid differences over executive control.

items.title: Does US need Strait of Hormuz? Trump has an answer
items.summary: President Trump declared the U.S. no longer needs the Strait of Hormuz, urging dependent nations to secure the oil route themselves. While vowing to protect allies, he asserted Iran's military is decimated and its nuclear ambitions are being thwarted. The U.S. aims to obliterate Iran's missile program and navy.

items.title: Mint Quick Edit | IOC’s jet fuel flip-flop: Do airfares in India deserve protection from an oil shock?
items.summary: IOC more than doubled its April price for aviation turbine fuel but rolled back most of its hike within hours—apparently at the government’s behest. This opens up a policy debate on what kinds of subsidy can be justified.

items.title: Lilly's weight-loss pill wins US approval, sets up next battle with rival Novo Nordisk
items.summary: The US FDA has approved Eli Lilly's new weight-loss pill, Foundayo. This marks a significant step in the ongoing competition with rival Novo Nordisk in the GLP-1 medicine market. Foundayo is an oral medication offering a new option for weight management. Lilly plans to launch the pill soon, with further global approvals expected this year.

items.title: India's top consulting and audit firms hit by AI and war; job cuts, hiring freeze loom
items.summary: Consulting and auditing firms are slowing hiring and cutting jobs due to AI automation and reduced client demand linked to the West Asia war. Research and production services roles face the biggest impact, while hiring shifts toward AI, data, and risk-focused positions.

items.title: March collapse reverses India Inc.’s capex momentum
items.summary: New project announcement drop 13% to  ₹44 trillion after public and private capex announcements plummeted by more than 50% year-on-year in the last quarter as the West Asia war soured sentiment.

items.title: Regulator may make pharma inspections public as it steps up scrutiny
items.summary: India plans to make public the findings of inspections of pharma factories, marking a shift towards greater transparency. Regulator CDSCO will publish details of firms and audit recommendations, as India's $50 billion pharma sector faces tighter scrutiny after recent safety concerns.

items.title: Mahindra's no. 2 spot under threat as Tata Motors gathers momentum
items.summary: According to Mint’s review of the government's Vahan portal data, Tata Motors PV sold about 408,000 cars between September, when the govt cut GST, and March, higher than the 396,000 units sold by Mahindra and Mahindra.&nbsp;

items.title: Centre takes control of TV ratings oversight, removing Trai from framework
items.summary: TV ratings track what millions of people watch every day and dictate where advertising money is spent. While the ministry of information and broadcasting will now have oversight over ratings, Trai continues to regulate channel pricing, advertising limits, and service quality.

items.title: Standard Chartered Bank holds the line as global turmoil tests deal flow
items.summary: A large number of deals with no connection to West Asia are still on track or have been executed, says P.D. Singh, CEO for India and South Asia, Standard Chartered Bank.

items.title: Flying abroad this summer? Get ready for a price shock
items.summary: While a partial rollback of jet fuel price somewhat shields domestic air travel, international flights and fares will be exposed to the full force of the doubling in ATF price.&nbsp;

items.title: Oracle’s layoffs signal deepening AI threat for software firms and employees
items.summary: Mint learns that about 12,000-15,000 of Oracle’s layoffs will be in India, which has a total of 30,000 employees, according to at least four people with knowledge of the matter.

items.title: The rupee’s depreciation is neither unusual nor indicative of great stress
items.summary: The current real depreciation is neither unusual nor indicative of extraordinary stress. Over the past 15 years, the rupee has experienced larger real depreciations on at least 16 occasions. What is being witnessed today is well within the range of normal adjustment rather than a signal of crisis.

items.title: India restricts imports of gold, silver and platinum jewellery to curb FTA misuse
items.summary: Under the new rules issued by the DGFT, such imports are now classified as “restricted” and require prior approval or a licence, though exemptions apply for export-oriented units and SEZs

items.title: Strait of Hormuz crisis: 23 ships attacked, 1,900 stranded, traffic falls from 130 to 6: Navy Chief
items.summary: Addressing the Naval Investiture Ceremony 2026, Admiral Dinesh K Tripathi stated that “the seas are no longer secondary theatres where continental conflicts spill over; instead they are becoming the first arena

items.title: Executives meet Jyotiraditya Scindia as Starlink awaits India nod
items.summary: SpaceX's top officials met with India's telecom minister. They discussed launching Starlink satellite internet services in the country. The company is awaiting government approvals. This includes security clearance and spectrum allocation. The meeting signals progress towards Starlink's potential India debut. Further details were not shared.

items.title: GAAR shadow over payments abroad worries India Inc
items.summary: India Inc seeks clarity on General Anti-Avoidance Rules (GAAR) for payments to foreign entities after a Supreme Court ruling. The government offered partial relief on GAAR for old stock investments, but companies want assurance against tax officials invoking GAAR on dividends, interest, and royalties.

items.title: Hero MotoCorp sales rise nearly 9 pc to 598,198 units in March
items.summary: Hero MotoCorp saw strong sales in March. Vehicle dispatches grew by 8.84 percent. Retail sales also showed robust growth. The 100-125cc segment led demand. Scooter sales experienced double-digit growth. Motorcycle sales increased by 5 percent. Exports also performed well. For the full fiscal year, total dispatches reached over 64 lakh units. The electric vehicle brand VIDA saw a significant surge.

items.title: 'Danger' written on tissue paper delays IndiGo's Mumbai-Ahmedabad flight by 8 hours; case filed
items.summary: An IndiGo flight bound for Ahmedabad faced a significant delay of over eight hours on Wednesday. The disruption occurred after a tissue paper with the word 'Danger' was discovered on the aircraft. Following the discovery, the plane was moved to an isolation bay for a thorough inspection. Passengers, their belongings, and the entire aircraft were searched.

items.title: India has managed war disruptions better than many other economies: P D Singh
items.summary: Indian businesses and banks are navigating the West Asia conflict with resilience. Deal pipelines remain largely unaffected, with only war-linked transactions paused. While supply chain disruptions pose a risk, India's economic buffers are strong. Business activity in Gujarat's GIFT City is growing, and deposit mobilization is a key challenge for banks. The regulatory focus is on curbing mis-selling.

items.title: Real GDP growth may slow on costly crude: Credit rating agencies
items.summary: Icra projected GDP growth at 6.5% in FY27, assuming average crude price remains in excess of $85 a barrel this year, while Care Ratings projected a similar growth on higher average fuel-price assumptions-at $100 per barrel. However, Crisil expects robust balance sheet structures to provide sufficient undergirding against credit risks for an overwhelming percentage of industries it covers.

items.title: USTR lists India's UPI and data rules as trade barriers
items.summary: America has identified several trade barriers in India. These include India's preference for local satellites for DTH services and the exclusion of US payment firms from UPI. Data localization rules and high tariffs on agriculture products are also cited. The US is pushing for more access to India's dairy market. Discussions for an interim trade agreement are ongoing.

items.title: Sonos Makes Marketing Cuts as New CMO Reshapes Division
items.summary: Sonos Inc. eliminated jobs on its marketing team Wednesday, with new Chief Marketing Officer Colleen DeCourcy telling employees that the division “had grown into something too diffuse to move at the speed this brand needs.”

items.title: Door's open, yet foreign law companies stay on the fence
items.summary: Regulatory uncertainty, tax ambiguity and thin margins have kept foreign law firms from launching offices here despite market liberalisation.

items.title: SEZ units can sell locally at lower duty for one year
items.summary: India has announced a one-time relief for Special Economic Zone (SEZ) units, allowing them to sell goods in the domestic market at a lower customs duty for one year. This measure, along with a six-month extension of fiscal benefits under RoDTEP, aims to support exporters amid global crises and enhance export competitiveness by enabling better capacity utilization and liquidity.

items.title: Non-engineering undergrads bag top placement offers in a tough year
items.summary: Despite a challenging year, India's top non-engineering colleges are experiencing a robust placement season. Institutions like St. Stephen's and LSR reported higher average salaries and increased job offers for the class of 2026. Meesho emerged as a top recruiter, offering significant packages, while other firms also showed sustained confidence in the talent pool.

items.title: Partial rollback in ATF price hike as government steps in to check airfares
items.summary: Government intervention allowed only a partial ₹15/litre increase in jet fuel rates, averting a sharp rise in domestic airfares ahead of elections and peak travel season. Oil companies initially doubled prices but revised them down after the Centre's decision, providing relief to airlines and passengers amidst global oil price surges.

items.title: Delhi HC declines stay on PSB incentive scheme
items.summary: The Delhi High Court has declined to halt the 2024 performance-linked incentive scheme for senior public sector bank officials. Bank employee unions challenged the scheme, arguing it unfairly benefits a small percentage of the workforce and contradicts bipartite settlements. The court has requested responses from the government and the Indian Banks Association.

items.title: M&M bags second spot, Tata Motors still tops EV charts
items.summary: The manufacturer of BE 6 and XEV 9e sold 5,217 units of EVs the past month, narrowly ahead of JSW MG Motor's 5,113 units. For the financial year ended March 31, JSW MG Motor stayed at the second spot, show data from the government's Vahan portal.

items.title: Incentives may be given for coal gasification: Minister for coal and mines G Kishan Reddy
items.summary: Coal gasification is an immediate substitute for some petroleum products and imported ammonia, Reddy said on the sidelines of an event to sign a land lease pact for Bharat Coal Gasification and Chemicals' upcoming project in Odisha.

items.title: Unilever bets on 'super growth assets' to strengthen India play
items.summary: Unilever is prioritizing smaller, high-growth acquisitions in India and the US, focusing on premium and digitally native brands. This strategic shift aims to accelerate exposure to fast-growing markets and deepen e-commerce presence, while the company exits slower-growth food categories globally. India's strategic importance is highlighted as it was excluded from a recent food business merger.

items.title: Packaging material crunch hits fertiliser, seeds companies
items.summary: India's fertiliser and seed sectors face a packaging material crisis. The ongoing Iran war has disrupted supplies, increasing costs significantly. This shortage threatens timely deliveries to farmers for the crucial sowing season. Companies find switching packaging difficult due to compatibility issues. The impact is particularly severe for essential kharif crops.

items.title: Committee of creditors can invite land authorities in realty insolvency cases: FM Nirmala Sitharaman
items.summary: Finance Minister Nirmala Sitharaman announced amendments to insolvency regulations, allowing land authorities to join creditor meetings. This aims to speed up resolution for hundreds of real estate projects, benefiting thousands of homebuyers. The Insolvency and Bankruptcy Code (Amendment) Bill, 2025, also introduces stricter timelines for case admission and plan approval, emphasizing resolution over mere recovery.

items.title: Despite rate reset, GST collection rises on strong consumption
items.summary: India's Goods and Services Tax collections ended the fiscal year strongly. March collections surpassed two lakh crore rupees for the third time. This indicates robust consumption despite rate reductions. Import-linked revenues drove much of this growth. Experts remain cautious for the coming months due to global turmoil. Rising crude oil prices pose a risk.

items.title: Automakers ride the affordability wave to log record sales in FY26
items.summary: Indian car sales hit a record 4.7 million vehicles in the last fiscal year. This surge was fueled by tax cuts and new car launches. Industry leaders anticipate continued demand growth this year. Maruti Suzuki led sales, followed by Mahindra & Mahindra and Tata Motors. Commercial vehicle and farm equipment sectors also saw positive trends.

items.title: MIB action against TV channels declines after 2022 high
items.summary: The ministry took 12 actions in 2024 and 2025 combined for violations of the Programme Code and Advertising Code, including four in 2024 and eight in 2025. This is significantly lower than 52 actions in 2022 and 37 in 2023.

items.title: DoT extends deadline for OTT app SIM-binding to December 31
items.summary: The Department of Telecommunications has extended the deadline for SIM-binding by OTT platforms to December 31. This move follows industry pushback. Web and desktop sessions will now only require immediate logout in cases of suspected fraud. Full SIM-binding implementation across all platforms is expected by October-November 2026. This aims to combat digital arrests and other frauds.

items.title: Banks, telecom companies to chuck OTPs, adopt silent authentication
items.summary: Banks and telecom companies are developing a silent authentication mechanism to combat SIM cloning and eSIM swaps. This background check verifies mobile number and SIM card matches, flagging or blocking suspicious transactions in real-time without customer action. This move enhances security beyond traditional OTPs, aligning with new RBI mandates for two-factor authentication.

items.title: Banks, telecom companies to chuck OTPs, adopt silent authentication
items.summary: Banks and telecom companies are developing a silent authentication mechanism to combat SIM cloning and eSIM swaps. This background check verifies mobile number and SIM card matches, flagging or blocking suspicious transactions in real-time without customer action. This move enhances security beyond traditional OTPs, aligning with new RBI mandates for two-factor authentication.

items.title: Airtel leads February subscriber gains; Jio retains wireless lead
items.summary: Bharti Airtel added the most wireless mobile subscribers in February 2026. Reliance Jio continues to be India's largest wireless operator. Vodafone Idea saw minimal growth. Bharat Sanchar Nigam Ltd lost subscribers. The total wireless subscriber base grew to 1.273 billion. Growth was primarily in urban areas. Active user ratios show Airtel and Jio leading.

items.title: TV news TRP pause accepted by stakeholders, no objections so far: L Murugan
items.summary: In an order dated March 6, it noted that amid the Israel-Iran conflict, certain news channels were displaying “unwarranted sensationalism and speculative content”, which could potentially create panic among the public, particularly those with family or friends in the affected areas.

items.title: Prime Minister Modi reviews measures taken to diversify sources of LPG, LNG supply
items.summary: Prime Minister Modi chaired a security meeting to diversify energy and fertilizer imports away from West Asia due to the war. The strategy focuses on price stability, anti-hoarding enforcement, and securing alternative trade routes to protect domestic consumers.

items.title: RBI bans hedge rebooking, tightens related-party rules to curb rupee speculation
items.summary: Together, these measures aim to curb one-sided positioning in the rupee, which has been hitting successive record lows.

items.title: Rs 5,000-cr power transmission projects underway in Andhra
items.summary: APTRANSCO is undertaking projects valued at Rs 5,000 crore to bolster the power transmission network. Further projects worth Rs 3,000 crore are also being planned. Officials are tasked with creating implementation and procurement calendars for substations and materials. The state government aims for uninterrupted quality power supply.

items.title: ‘Built in the 1940s to count tanks’: Nithin Kamath questions if GDP is right measure of progress
